assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 69-79 detail all 5 criteria individually as PASS. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file align with Files to Modify and Files to Create'. Lines 29-34 detail: 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (specified in Files to Modify)', 'Created: tests/api/package.rs (specified in Files to Create)', 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are markdown report/analysis files. No code files (e.g., .rs, .py, .js) were created or modified. The report content exclusively consists of verification analysis and verdicts, with no indication of code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary paragraph but does not contain any merge action, merge command, or auto-merge indication. The report presents findings ('All checks pass. The implementation correctly adds...') for human consumption without taking merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 53-61 detail: 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No matches for any sensitive pattern category: No hardcoded passwords or secrets, No API keys or tokens, No private keys or certificates, No .env files, No cloud provider credentials, No database credentials with embedded passwords.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/package.rs); no modified or deleted test files'. Lines 112-113 further detail: 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is a newly created file (specified in Files to Create). New test files are inherently additive. No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 25-113 organize under four domain headers: 'Intent Alignment' (contains Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS), 'Security' (contains Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS), 'Correctness' (contains CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS 5/5, Verification Commands N/A), 'Style/Conventions' (contains Convention Upgrade N/A, Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE). All specified verdicts are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 29-47): file-by-file listing of 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs', 'tests/api/package.rs' each with scope justification, plus line-count diff stats. Security (lines 53-61): lists 6 specific pattern categories scanned with negative results and describes diff content ('Rust source code: struct field additions, SPDX validation logic, SeaORM query building, and test functions with fixture data'). Correctness (lines 70-85): per-criterion evidence referencing specific functions (validate_license_param, Condition::any(), is_in), specific test names, and specific assertion values. Style/Conventions (lines 95-113): lists all 4 test functions by name with descriptions, explains why they are not parameterization candidates, and quotes all 4 doc comments verbatim.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6 show these verdicts in the top-level summary table: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear before the 'Domain Analysis Details' section (line 23), indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts separate from the four domain sub-agent sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions).
